page: Microsoft Azure B4pls_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Bpsv2 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an ARM64 architecture powered by an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor. The server is configured with 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz with a single thread per core. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates that the B4pls_v2 delivers entry-level to moderate performance. In stress-ng testing, the single-core CPU performance scored 1822.19 and multi-core scored 7243.95, both falling within the average range. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is average, with read-only speeds at 48.64 GB/sec and write speeds at 29.1 GB/sec, though small block cached read-only bandwidth is weak at 283.03 GB/sec. The server exhibits weak performance in Geekbench tests, scoring 1099.0 for single-core and 3672.0 for multi-core. PassMark CPU and memory scores are also low, with the memory score of 1078.78 categorized as poor. Cryptographic performance is limited, as shown by a poor PassMark encryption score of 646.54, despite having AES hardware acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the B4pls_v2 utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model, making it a budget-friendly option for workloads that do not require continuous full CPU utilization. The primary tradeoff is the low memory-to-vCPU ratio and weak overall performance in compute-intensive tasks, such as LLM inference where it achieves poor token generation speeds. However, it maintains average performance in database operations and gzip compression. This server is suited for low-traffic web serving, lightweight database operations, development or testing environments, and basic background tasks that benefit from a burstable ARM64 architecture.
description: Microsoft Azure B4pls_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Bpsv2 family, built on the ARM64 architecture using an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it provides 4 physical cores (4 vCPUs) at 3.0 GHz and 8.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a low-memory profile of 2.0 GB per core. The instance includes no local storage or GPUs but supports premium storage. Performance benchmarks place this server in the entry-level tier, with average stress-ng CPU scores but weak Geekbench and PassMark memory results. Its burstable CPU allocation offers qualitative cost efficiency for intermittent workloads, making it a budget-friendly choice. The B4pls_v2 is suited for lightweight database operations, basic web serving, and development environments that do not require sustained high-performance compute.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B4pls_v2 is a burstable ARM64 virtual machine featuring 4 Ampere Neoverse-N1 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for budget-friendly, low-intensity workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B4pls_v2 is a burstable ARM64 virtual machine with 4 Ampere vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, configured for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, burstable ARM64 virtual machine with four cores and low memory for lightweight, intermittent workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor at 3.0 GHz
Equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM and no bundled local storage
Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model for cost-efficient operation
Delivers average performance in database operations and gzip compression
Suited for lightweight databases, development environments, and basic web serving
